title: Rose geranium and frankincense reed diffuser
subtitle: A 100 ml reed diffuser with dipropylene glycol, rose geranium, and frankincense essential oils
excerpt: Dipropylene glycol (DPG) is the standard carrier for reed diffusers: it draws essential oils up through the reeds by capillary action and releases the fragrance slowly into a room. Rose geranium gives a rosy, slightly herbal character; frankincense adds a warm, resinous base. The two together give a sophisticated fragrance that suits a living room or home office. Yield is 100 ml.
sourceNotes: Reed diffuser mechanics: capillary action draws the DPG and EO blend up through the rattan reeds; evaporation from the top of the reeds releases the fragrance. DPG (dipropylene glycol) is the industry-standard carrier because of its low viscosity and slow evaporation rate. EO usage rate in reed diffusers: 10 to 30% is the typical range; 15% used here gives a moderate to strong throw. Rose geranium EO (Pelargonium graveolens): rosy, slightly herbal and citrus. Frankincense EO (Boswellia carterii): warm, resinous, slow-releasing base note. Both are well-suited to reed diffusers as they have moderate to good diffusion rates.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One reed diffuser bottle and reed set per 100 ml batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Insert the reeds and flip them once to saturate.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 to 4 months of continuous diffusing before the blend is exhausted. The DPG base itself does not degrade. Unopened, the blend keeps 12 months in a sealed bottle.

glossaryTerms.definition: The process by which a liquid moves up through a narrow tube or porous material against gravity, caused by adhesion between the liquid and the tube walls. In a reed diffuser, capillary action draws the DPG and essential oil blend up through the rattan reeds to the tip, where evaporation releases the fragrance.

A synthetic glycol used as the carrier liquid in reed diffusers. Its low viscosity and low vapour pressure allow it to wick up through rattan reeds efficiently and release fragrance slowly, making it the standard carrier for home reed diffusers.

content.text: : rattan reeds draw the liquid up to their tips, where it evaporates slowly into the room air.

is the standard carrier: it wicks well through the reeds and evaporates slowly, extending the diffuser life. Rose geranium gives a rosy, herbal mid note; frankincense adds a warm, resinous base that helps slow evaporation. Place the diffuser in a room with some air movement; strong draughts speed it up and shorten the life.

items.prepNote: Standard carrier for reed diffusers; cosmetic or fragrance grade
8% of total weight; rosy, slightly herbal
7% of total weight; warm, resinous base note

content.text: Weigh the dipropylene glycol into a small jug. Add the rose geranium and frankincense essential oils and stir to combine; they will dissolve readily in the DPG. Pour into the reed diffuser bottle using the funnel. Insert the diffuser reeds. Flip the reeds once immediately to saturate the top ends with the blend and start diffusing.

Place the diffuser on a stable surface where it will not be knocked over. Flip the reeds every 2 to 3 days to refresh the fragrance throw; more frequent flipping speeds up evaporation and shortens the diffuser life. Position away from strong draughts and direct sunlight, which accelerate evaporation. Replace the reeds when the diffuser is refilled; old reeds can become clogged and lose wicking efficiency.
A 100 ml diffuser lasts 3 to 4 months with regular reed flipping. The DPG base does not degrade; any unused blend in a sealed bottle keeps 12 months. Replace the reeds when refilling.
